title: validate-rules prints full Go stack traces to stderr on validation failure (use %v not %+v)
summary: When alert-rule validation fails, cos-tool prints the full Go stack trace to stderr (which then floods `juju debug-log`). Root cause is the `%+v` format verb applied to errors that come from `github.com/pkg/errors`: such errors implement a Formatter that, under `%+v`, renders the entire call stack. The offending lines are pkg/tool/promql_transform.go:26 and pkg/tool/logql_transform.go:22 -- both `return rg, fmt.Errorf("error validating %s: %+v", filename, errs)` -- and pkg/lokiruler/compat.go:23 `errs[i] = fmt.Errorf("%+v", errs[i])`, which wraps each Loki parse error with a stack-trace-bearing pkg/errors value. Switching `%+v` to `%v` (or `%s`) yields the concise one-line messages the README already advertises (e.g. 'error validating rule_file.yaml: [5:15: ...]').
why_good_first_issue: Single well-understood root cause with a tiny, low-risk diff (change `%+v` to `%v` in 2-3 lines). Maintainer-filed bug with a clear, user-visible symptom and an explicit 'expected behavior: user-friendly error messages without Go stacks'. The README at line 151-155 already shows the desired concise format, giving the contributor an exact target. Existing tests in pkg/tool validate the rule-error path so a contributor can extend one to assert the message no longer contains 'goroutine'/file paths.
repro_steps: Go is NOT installed on VM (command -v go empty), so inspection tier. On VM cos: multipass exec cos -- git clone --depth 80 https://github.com/canonical/cos-tool ~/work/cos-tool; then `grep -rn '%+v' pkg/ cmd/ main.go` and read pkg/tool/promql_transform.go:20-29, pkg/lokiruler/compat.go:11-26. Cross-reference the stack trace in issue #21 which names pkg/tool/promql_transform.go:14 (ValidateRules) and cmd/root/root.go:77.
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection: `grep -rn '%+v'` returns exactly 3 hits -- pkg/tool/promql_transform.go:26, pkg/tool/logql_transform.go:22, pkg/lokiruler/compat.go:23. promql_transform.go:23-26 builds the error as `fmt.Errorf("error validating %s: %+v", filename, errs)` from rulefmt.Parse errors; compat.go imports `github.com/pkg/errors` (line 11) whose errors emit a stack trace under %+v. main.go:8-12 then does logrus `log.Fatal(err)` and root.go:84 returns `cli.Exit(err, 1)`, so the stack-laden string surfaces on stderr / juju debug-log exactly as in the issue. This is the precise mechanism behind the reported 'rulefmt.go:90 ... command.go:169 ...' trace.
fix_hint: Replace `%+v` with `%v` (or `%s`) in pkg/tool/promql_transform.go:26 and pkg/tool/logql_transform.go:22; in pkg/lokiruler/compat.go:23 drop the `fmt.Errorf("%+v", ...)` re-wrap entirely (or use `errors.Cause(errs[i])` / `%v`) so pkg/errors stack frames are not emitted. Add/extend a unit test in pkg/tool asserting the returned error string does not contain 'goroutine' or '.go:' line references. Optionally have main.go print to stderr without logrus's default formatting.
notes: Distinct, separate part of the issue: it also mentions a *duplicate group name* in generated rules (gft_model_..._alerts repeated) -- that is a charm-side rule-generation problem, NOT a cos-tool bug, so the cos-tool-scoped fix here is strictly the stack-trace formatting. Go absent so cannot `go test`; reproduction is inspection-tier with exact file:line + the issue's own stack trace as corroboration.

title: Add unit tests for untested packages (cmd/root and pkg/lokiruler are at 0% coverage)
suggested_labels: Type: Enhancement; good first issue; Status: Help Wanted
summary: The maintainer-filed issue lists several packages at 0.0% coverage. Excluding the vendored-from-Loki trees (pkg/logql/*), the genuinely cos-tool-owned untested code is the CLI wiring in cmd/root (cmd/root/root.go, 0.0%) and the Loki rule loader in pkg/lokiruler (pkg/lokiruler/compat.go, 0.0%). A beginner can add table-driven tests: for cmd/root, exercise the cli.App commands (transform / validate-rules / validate-config) and the Before hook that selects PromQL vs LogQL via the --format flag; for pkg/lokiruler, feed Load() valid and invalid rule YAML and assert the returned (*rulefmt.RuleGroups, []error).
why_good_first_issue: Pure additive work (no behaviour change), naturally scoped to a single package per PR, and Go's standard testing makes table-driven tests approachable. The repo already has good example tests in pkg/tool/*_test.go to copy the style from. Maintainer explicitly asked for this, so it is wanted and unlikely to be contested. Clear 'done': new _test.go files raising coverage for cmd/root and/or pkg/lokiruler above 0%.
repro_steps: Go is NOT installed on VM, so cannot run `go test ./... -coverprofile`. On VM cos: clone repo, then `find . -name '*_test.go' -not -path './pkg/logql/*'` (lists only pkg/tool/*) and `find cmd -name '*_test.go'` (empty) and `ls pkg/lokiruler` (only compat.go).
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection: the only non-vendored test files are six files under pkg/tool/ (logql_transform_test.go, promql_alert_test.go, promql_transform_test.go, promql_config_check_test.go, logql_alert_test.go, common_test.go). `find cmd -name '*_test.go'` returns nothing -> cmd/root/root.go is untested (matches the issue's 'cmd/root coverage: 0.0%'). pkg/lokiruler/ contains only compat.go, no test -> matches 'lokiruler 0.0%'. So the issue's coverage gaps are still present on current main; it is NOT obsolete.
fix_hint: Start with the smallest target: add pkg/lokiruler/compat_test.go calling lokiruler.Load() on a small valid rule set (expect 0 errors) and on a malformed one (expect non-empty []error). Then cmd/root/root_test.go: build the cli.App (or call Execute with crafted os.Args via a helper) and assert transform output and validate-rules exit behaviour, including the --format promql|logql branch in the Before hook. Mirror the table-driven structure already used in pkg/tool/promql_transform_test.go.
notes: Scope guidance for the contributor: do NOT target the pkg/logql/* packages the issue also lists (jsonexpr, logfmt, pattern, syntax, util, logqlmodel) -- those are vendored copies of Grafana Loki internals and are not the right place for cos-tool's own tests; focus on cmd/root and pkg/lokiruler. Go absent so coverage numbers were verified structurally (presence/absence of _test.go) rather than by running `go test`.
